title: Rail travel assistants
description: Handle baggage for travelers at transportation terminals or for guests at hotels or similar establishments. Pick up and deliver messages, documents, packages, and other items between offices or departments within an establishment or directly to other business concerns, traveling by foot, bicycle, motorcycle, automobile, or public conveyance. Supervise and coordinate the activities of ground crew in the loading, unloading, securing, and staging of aircraft cargo or baggage. May determine the quantity and orientation of cargo and compute aircraft center of gravity. May accompany aircraft as member of flight crew and monitor and handle cargo in flight, and assist and brief passengers on safety and emergency procedures. Includes loadmasters. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Provide boarding assistance to elderly, sick, or injured people and secure passengers for transportation by buckling seatbelts or fastening wheelchairs with tie-down straps.

Determine or facilitate seating arrangements and ensure proper ticket verification by counting and verifying tickets and seat reservations, and recording numbers of passengers boarding and disembarking.
Greet passengers boarding transportation equipment, announce routes and stops, and provide customers with information on routes, gates, prices, timetables, terminals, and concourses.
Deliver messages and room service orders, and run errands for guests, including providing travel information and directions to guests or travelers.
Conduct safety checks on equipment prior to departure and explain and demonstrate safety procedures and the use of safety equipment to passengers.
Transfer luggage, trunks, and packages to and from rooms, loading areas, vehicles, or transportation terminals, including receiving and marking baggage with claim checks.
Maintain clean lobbies or entrance areas for travelers or guests and inspect guests' rooms to ensure they are adequately stocked and comfortable.
Pick up and return items for laundry and valet service, and assist travelers and guests with disabilities.
Monitor safety procedures and equipment to maintain a safe working environment.
Supervise and coordinate the activities of ground crew during loading, unloading, securing, and staging of baggage or aircraft cargo, including determining cargo quantity and orientation.
Record information such as items received and delivered, recipients' responses to messages, and maintain logs or operating data as needed for proper documentation.
Operate equipment and vehicles necessary for transportation and delivery of materials, and perform routine maintenance on delivery vehicles and equipment.
Plan work schedules and assign duties to maintain adequate staff for effective performance, and counsel employees in work-related activities.
Collect, sort, and manage recyclable materials, while ensuring compliance with community ordinances.
Collaborate with workers and managers to solve work-related problems; review customer requests for service to determine service needs, and deploy appropriate resources.
Train new employees in areas such as safety procedures and equipment operation, and assess training needs of staff and provide appropriate instruction.
Prepare and maintain work records and reports of information such as employee time and wages, in addition to preparing bills of lading and statements of shipping records.

Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation and operational goals, and confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems.

Interpret transportation regulations, shipping orders, safety regulations, and company policies to workers, and review orders, production schedules, and shipping notices to determine work sequences and material shipping details.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities and operations, and maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities.
Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, and equipment to ensure safety and compliance with specifications, and examine cargo to determine specific handling requirements.
Drive vehicles or operate machinery to complete work assignments, and dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to emergency reports.
Recommend and implement measures to improve worker motivation and customer services, and train workers in proper operational procedures and job functions.
Conduct repairs or preventive maintenance on vehicles or equipment, and requisition needed personnel, supplies, or equipment.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends, and meet with managers to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
